name: Critical Security Tool Stopped
notes:
This rule triggers when permission of critical files or directories are modified. An attacker may modify the permissions of a sensitive file to become the only user able to access it and proceed to Defacement, Data Destruction, Disabling Security Tools, etc.

Note: The reference set Critical Security Tool Processes has been pre-populated with common security tool process names. Tune the reference set with any tool used by the organization.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Service Stopped
when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Critical Security Tool Processes - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY Critical Security Tool Stopped on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Service Stopped
AND when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Critical Security Tool Processes - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: Critical Security Tool Stopped
Event description: Detect when a critical security tool has been stopped.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
